questions.question: Briefly explain the difference between a Blind test and a Double-Blind test. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: A Blind test is one where the security team is aware the test is happening.
A Double-Blind test is one where the security team has no prior knowledge of the test.
The Double-Blind test is a more realistic measure of detection and response capabilities.

questions.question: A bank wants to test its defenses against a determined attacker who has already tricked an employee into installing malware. Which penetration test types (by knowledge and by environment) would be most appropriate and why? (5 marks)
expectedPoints: By Knowledge: A Grey-Box test is appropriate as it simulates an attacker with a foothold (e.g., low-privilege access).
By Environment: An Internal Network Penetration Test is key, as the attack is originating from inside the network.
The test should focus on privilege escalation techniques to gain higher access.
It should also focus on lateral movement to access critical systems (e.g., databases).
A Social Engineering assessment might also be recommended to test the human vulnerability that led to the initial breach.
